title: Playwright with Java - Zero to Automation: Introduction & Setup
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Get started with Playwright and Java from scratch. Learn what Playwright is, why it beats Selenium, and set up your first real project with Maven - including writing and running your first test.

title: AWS Compute: EC2, Lambda, ECS, and EKS
author: Abhishek-sinha
excerpt: A practical guide to AWS compute services — EC2 Auto Scaling, Lambda serverless functions, ECS Fargate containers, and EKS Kubernetes — with decision framework for choosing the right service.

title: AWS Storage & Databases: S3, RDS, DynamoDB, and ElastiCache
author: Abhishek-sinha
excerpt: A practical guide to AWS storage and database services — S3 deep dive with lifecycle policies, RDS Multi-AZ and Aurora, DynamoDB table design, and ElastiCache caching strategies.

title: AWS Networking & Security: VPC Deep Dive, Route53, CloudFront, and WAF
author: Abhishek-sinha
excerpt: A deep dive into AWS networking and security — advanced VPC patterns with Transit Gateway and VPC Endpoints, Route53 routing policies and DNS failover, CloudFront content delivery with OAI, and WAF/Shield application protection.

title: AWS Production: High Availability, Monitoring, Cost Optimization, and CI/CD
author: Abhishek-sinha
excerpt: A comprehensive guide to production-ready AWS — Multi-AZ high availability architectures, CloudWatch monitoring with the four golden signals, cost optimization with Savings Plans and lifecycle policies, and automated CI/CD pipelines with blue/green deployments.

title: AI Usage in Mobile Automation: Beyond Basic Test Scripts
excerpt: AI in mobile automation isn't just about generating tests-it's about creating intelligent, self-healing systems that adapt to app changes while reducing maintenance overhead by up to 80%. Mobile application testing has reached a critical inflection point.

title: Setting Up Your Local Sandbox: Running the Mock API Server via Node.js
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: When you're diving into API testing—whether you're using RestAssured, Supertest, Postman, or Karate—the absolute best way to learn is by having your own local sandbox.

title: Dockerizing Your Test Environment: Running the Mock Server via Docker
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In the previous blog, we successfully set up our local mock API server using Node.js. While that works perfectly, configuring Node.js and managing dependencies can sometimes cause headaches.

title: Understanding Core REST: Fetching and Filtering API Data (GET)
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: At the heart of every API test automation framework lies the GET request. In this tutorial, we will explore the core REST endpoints provided by the Mock API Server and extract data using query parameters.

title: Modifying Data: Testing POST, PUT, and DELETE API Endpoints
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Real-world applications don't just read data; they constantly create, update, and delete records. In this post, we will explore the core methods used to modify data in REST architectures: POST, PUT, and DELETE.

title: Mastering Pagination and Sorting in REST API Tests
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In real-world applications, databases hold millions of records. To prevent servers from crashing, APIs return data in small, manageable chunks called pages. In this tutorial, we will learn how to master pagination and sorting.

title: Securing APIs: Testing JWT Authentication Workflows
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In a real-world production environment, you cannot let anyone access sensitive data. Modern APIs use security mechanisms like JSON Web Tokens (JWT) to ensure only authorized users can perform actions.

title: Chaos Engineering: Simulating Latency and Server Timeouts
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In the real world, servers get overloaded and network connections drop. In this tutorial, we will perform Chaos Engineering by intentionally sabotaging our own API requests to simulate severe latency.

title: Beyond REST: An Introduction to GraphQL Querying
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: REST is fantastic, but it has a massive flaw: over-fetching data. Enter GraphQL, a modern query language that allows clients to ask the server for exactly what they need, and nothing more.

title: Advanced GraphQL: Using Variables and Dynamic Filters
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Real applications don't just fetch static data. They fetch dynamic data based on user input. In this tutorial, we will learn the correct way to pass dynamic data into our queries using GraphQL Variables.

title: GraphQL Mutations: Creating and Updating Data
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In REST, we use specific HTTP methods like POST and PUT to modify data. In GraphQL, everything goes through a single endpoint. To modify data, GraphQL introduces the powerful concept of a Mutation.

title: Real-Time Testing: Connecting to WebSockets
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: What if you are testing a live chat application or a stock ticker? To solve this, modern applications use WebSockets. In this tutorial, we will connect to the server's built-in WebSocket chat room.

title: Webhooks: Testing Asynchronous Event Callbacks
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Keeping a WebSocket open 24/7 for a single, rare event is a massive waste of server resources. Instead, modern architectures use Webhooks to trigger asynchronous background events and listen for callbacks.

title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration: Running Tests on Every Commit
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: An automation script is useless if it only runs on your local laptop. In this tutorial, we will pull everything together by integrating our tests directly into a GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline!

title: Capstone Project: Building an End-to-End Test Suite
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: It is now time to prove everything you have learned. In this final Capstone Project, you will build a comprehensive test suite that touches every feature of the mock server in a single execution flow.

title: Testing Real-Time WebSocket Connections in Postman
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Postman isn't just for HTTP requests. It has robust, native support for WebSockets. In this tutorial, we will use Postman to connect to a live WebSocket server.

title: Automating GraphQL Queries and Mutations in Postman
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: GraphQL solves over-fetching by allowing explicit queries. In this tutorial, we will execute GraphQL Queries and Mutations natively within Postman.

title: End-to-End Postman Capstone
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: To prove that you are an API Automation Master, it is time to build a comprehensive, portfolio-grade End-to-End Postman Collection.

title: Introduction to REST APIs & HTTP Methods
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Before we write a single line of Java code, we must understand the language of the web. Modern web applications communicate through REST APIs.

title: Setting up RestAssured with Java
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Now that we understand how REST APIs work, it's time to build our automation framework using Java 17, RestAssured, and TestNG.

title: Writing your First GET/POST Request
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: In this tutorial, we will use RestAssured to extract information from our MyCodeYatra Mock API Server using a GET request, and then we will create entirely new data using a POST request.

title: Handling Query Parameters & Path Variables
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Real-world API testing requires dynamic URL construction. In this tutorial, we will use RestAssured to inject Query Parameters and Path Variables dynamically.

title: Advanced JSON Parsing with Jackson
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master API automation by serializing and deserializing JSON payloads dynamically using the powerful Jackson library in RestAssured.

title: API Authentication and Authorization
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to securely automate API endpoints by handling Basic Auth and Bearer Tokens using RestAssured's built-in authentication mechanisms.

title: Parsing and Validating XML Responses
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to effortlessly parse, navigate, and validate XML payloads in legacy or enterprise APIs using RestAssured's built-in xmlPath() tools.

title: Handling File Uploads and Downloads
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Discover how to automate file upload forms using multipart/form-data and successfully download and parse CSVs and binary files with RestAssured.

title: Testing GraphQL APIs with RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Demystify GraphQL testing by learning how to automate Queries and Mutations seamlessly using RestAssured's powerful JSON processing capabilities.

title: Generating Allure Reports with RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Elevate your API automation framework by integrating Allure Reports with TestNG and RestAssured to automatically capture and display HTTP request and response payloads.

title: Data-Driven API Testing with RestAssured and TestNG
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master Data-Driven Testing (DDT) by integrating TestNG DataProviders into your RestAssured framework to execute massive datasets through a single automated script.

title: Deserializing APIs into POJOs
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to eliminate raw JSON string parsing by automatically mapping HTTP API responses directly into strongly-typed Java objects using POJOs and Lombok.

title: JSON Schema Validation in RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Replace dozens of fragile assertions by learning how to validate entire API payload structures and data types using RestAssured's JSON Schema Validator.

title: Mocking APIs with WireMock and RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Eliminate flaky automated tests by simulating third-party HTTP dependencies locally using WireMock's powerful stubbing and verification capabilities.

title: RestAssured in CI/CD Pipelines
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to integrate your automated RestAssured API test suites into GitHub Actions and Jenkins pipelines to guarantee production stability.

title: Introduction to Observability-Driven Testing
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Move beyond traditional Black-Box API testing. Learn the core concepts of Observability-Driven Testing (ODT) and how asserting distributed traces is revolutionizing QA.

title: OpenTelemetry Integration with RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to inject W3C Trace Context headers into your RestAssured tests to initiate distributed OpenTelemetry traces across your backend microservices.

title: Tracetest Deep Dive with RestAssured
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: The grand finale! Master Observability-Driven Testing by writing fluent assertions against your distributed backend databases and message queues using Tracetest.

title: Introduction to SuperTest & Express.js
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Kickstart your NodeJS API Testing journey! Learn what SuperTest is, why it's the industry standard, and write your first asynchronous GET request using Jest and TypeScript.

title: Setting up Supertest with Jest/Vitest
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to build a robust NodeJS API testing framework from scratch using SuperTest, Jest, and TypeScript, including powerful testing hooks.

title: Testing CRUD Operations
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master the core of API testing by writing an End-to-End Test Suite that sequentially validates the entire Create, Read, Update, and Delete lifecycle.

title: TypeScript Interfaces for Payloads
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to transform SuperTest into an enterprise-grade framework by enforcing strict typings on your API Requests using TypeScript Interfaces.

title: Handling Async/Await & Promises
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Prevent flaky tests by mastering NodeJS asynchronous execution. Learn how to handle Promises, Async/Await, and parallel HTTP requests in SuperTest.

title: Authentication & Authorization
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to bypass security gateways in your test automation suite by mastering Basic Auth and dynamic JWT Bearer Tokens in SuperTest.

title: Testing GraphQL APIs
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Demystify GraphQL test automation. Learn how to execute Queries and Mutations by passing schema strings into SuperTest HTTP POST payloads.

title: Testing WebSocket Connections
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to overcome SuperTest's HTTP limitations by integrating the 'ws' library to automate real-time WebSocket communication in Jest.

title: Testing File Uploads & Downloads
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master binary payloads and multipart form boundaries. Learn how to effortlessly test file uploads and downloads using SuperTest.

title: Handling Request Timeouts & Chaos Testing
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Ensure your CI/CD pipeline never hangs again. Master SuperTest timeout controls and learn how to test against unexpected API failures.

title: Integrating Faker.js for Test Data
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Eliminate brittle hardcoded payloads. Learn how to integrate Faker.js with SuperTest to generate massive amounts of dynamic, realistic API test data.

title: Setting up Global Setup & Teardown Hooks
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Architect scalable automation frameworks by mastering Jest lifecycle hooks. Learn how to manage state, databases, and Auth tokens efficiently across SuperTest suites.

title: Mocking Axios / Fetch Requests
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Keep your API tests fast, deterministic, and free from third-party rate limits. Learn how to intercept outbound network requests using jest.mock().

title: Generating Test Coverage Reports
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Stop guessing if your APIs are fully tested. Learn how to generate beautiful tabular test coverage reports using Jest and SuperTest to catch missing edge cases.

title: Configuring CI/CD for Jest/SuperTest
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Never merge a broken API again. Learn how to configure GitHub Actions to automatically execute your SuperTest suite on every pull request.

title: Best Practices & Folder Architecture
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Scale your API testing frameworks to thousands of tests. Discover the ultimate enterprise folder architecture and coding standards for SuperTest suites.

title: Introduction to Karate DSL
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Welcome to the Karate API Testing Series! Enter the realm of Behavior Driven Development (BDD) with Karate DSL and write your very first feature file.

title: Setting up Karate and Environment Switching
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Learn how to structure your Karate repository for the real world! Configure dynamic environments using karate-config.js and execute folder-specific test runners.

title: JSON and XML Expressions
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Discover the true power of Karate DSL! Learn how to assert complex JSON objects and deeply nested XML responses natively without writing a single Java POJO.

title: Calling other Feature Files
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Keep your testing codebase DRY! Learn how to create reusable authentication scripts in Karate and dynamically pass variables between multiple feature files.

title: Data Driven Testing with CSVs
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Master Data-Driven Testing in Karate! Learn how to dynamically execute Scenarios using external CSV datasets without writing a single line of parsing code.

title: Reading JSON Payloads from Files
author: pankaj-kumar
excerpt: Keep your Karate feature files clean! Learn how to read external JSON files natively, dynamically mutate their contents, and use them as API request payloads.
